stories.title: Project Collaboration
stories.description:
Enable real-time collaboration on projects between ToDoozy users. Projects are personal/local by default and become shared when the owner generates an invite link. Shared project data syncs through Supabase Postgres with Realtime subscriptions.

## Architecture Overview

Hybrid sync model: personal projects stay in local SQLite, shared projects write-through to both local SQLite and Supabase Postgres. Supabase Realtime pushes changes to all connected members instantly. Offline edits queue locally and sync on reconnect with last-write-wins at the field level.

## Supabase Schema (New Tables with RLS)

Create these tables in Supabase Postgres with Row Level Security policies that enforce project membership:

- `shared_projects` — mirrors local project fields (id, name, description, color, icon, owner_id, created_at, updated_at)
- `shared_project_members` — project_id, user_id, role ('owner'|'member'), joined_at
- `shared_project_invites` — token (UUID), project_id, created_by, expires_at (15 min from creation), accepted_by, status ('pending'|'accepted'|'expired'). Auto-cleanup expired tokens.
- `shared_tasks` — mirrors local task fields (all columns from tasks table)
- `shared_statuses` — mirrors local status fields for the project
- `shared_activity_log` — mirrors activity_log for the project

RLS policies: users can only SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE rows in projects where they have a row in shared_project_members.

## Sharing Flow (Converting Personal → Shared)

1. Owner clicks Share icon on an unshared project in the sidebar/header
2. App uploads project data (project, tasks, subtasks, statuses, activity log) to Supabase
3. Creates owner's entry in shared_project_members with role='owner'
4. Generates first invite link (UUID token, 15-min expiry) and copies to clipboard
5. Shows toast: 'Project shared! Invite link copied to clipboard.'
6. Sidebar shows shared indicator icon on the project
7. From this point, all writes go to both local SQLite and Supabase (write-through)

## Invite Link Flow

- Format: `todoozy://invite/{token}`
- Register `todoozy://` as Electron custom protocol handler
- When app receives the deep link:
  - Validate token against Supabase shared_project_invites
  - If valid and not expired: show dialog 'Join [Project Name] by [Owner Name]? Accept / Decline'
  - If expired: show 'This invite link has expired. Ask the project owner for a new one.'
  - On accept: add user to shared_project_members, sync project data down, project appears in sidebar
  - On decline: dismiss dialog, no changes
- Owner can generate multiple invite links (each with own 15-min window)
- Expired tokens are cleaned up automatically (delete on query or periodic cleanup)

## Roles & Permissions

- **Owner**: Full control — project settings (name, color, icon), invite/remove members, generate invite links, unshare project. Can manage tasks.
- **Member**: Can create, edit, delete, assign tasks and subtasks. Cannot modify project settings, membership, or unshare.

## UI Changes

### Sidebar
- Shared projects show a small 'shared' indicator icon (e.g., Users icon from lucide-react, 12px) next to the project name
- No avatar circles in sidebar — keep it clean

### Middle Pane — Project Header
- For shared projects, show Asana-style overlapping avatar circles in the header area:
  - Each member (excluding the current user) gets a circle with their initials (from display_name or email)
  - Colors assigned by join order from a rotating palette
  - Hover shows email tooltip
  - Overflow: show first few circles + '+N' badge
  - Click avatar area opens Settings > Members
- Share icon visible for unshared projects (click to share)
- Trash/delete icon moves to end of avatar circles row

### Project Settings > Members Section
- Full member list: avatar circle, email address, role badge (Owner/Member)
- 'Generate Invite Link' button — creates 15-min token, copies to clipboard, shows toast
- Remove button per member (owner only) — removes member from Supabase, member gets notified
- 'Leave Project' button (members only) — shows dialog: 'Delete project or keep a local copy?'
- 'Unshare' button (owner only) — confirmation dialog: 'This will remove all members. They can keep a local copy. Unshare?' On confirm: sends real-time event to all members, each gets 'Delete or keep local copy?' dialog. Supabase data deleted, owner's project reverts to local-only.

### Task Assignment
- In shared projects, the assignee picker shows project members with avatar circles + emails
- assigned_to field stores user ID from shared_project_members

## Real-Time Sync

- Subscribe to Supabase Realtime channels for each shared project the user is a member of
- Listen for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on shared_tasks, shared_statuses, shared_activity_log, shared_project_members
- On receiving events: update local SQLite and Zustand stores
- On project unshare event: show 'Delete or keep local copy?' dialog to all members
- On member removal event: show same dialog to the removed member

## Offline Behavior

- Shared projects are editable offline using local SQLite copy
- Changes queue in a local `sync_queue` table (table_name, row_id, operation, payload, created_at)
- On reconnect: push queued changes to Supabase in order, last-write-wins at field level
- Show subtle 'Offline — changes will sync when reconnected' indicator in project header

## Labels

- Labels stay personal — each user has their own label colors
- When syncing tasks, store label NAME (not ID) in Supabase task data
- When receiving a synced task with a label name the user doesn't have locally: auto-create the label with a default color
- Each user sees their own colors for the same label name

## My Day

- Tasks from shared projects can appear in My Day
- Adding a task to My Day is per-user (stored locally in my_day_tasks with user_id)
- Does NOT affect other members' My Day

## Notifications

- New local SQLite table: `notifications` (id, type, message, task_id, project_id, from_user_id, read, created_at)
- Bell icon in app header with unread count badge
- Click bell → notification panel flyout listing recent notifications
- Click a notification → navigates to the relevant task/project, marks as read
- Toast fires for real-time events when online (assignments)
- When a task is assigned to a user in a shared project: create notification for the assignee
- Offline assignments: create notifications on reconnect sync
- Scope: task assignments only for now (extensible for future event types)

## Member Removal / Leaving

- Removed member (by owner): real-time event triggers 'Delete or keep local copy?' dialog
- Member leaves voluntarily: same dialog
- If member was offline when removed: on reconnect, Supabase RLS rejects sync → app detects and shows same dialog
- 'Keep local copy': project stays in local SQLite, becomes personal/unshared, no more sync
- 'Delete': project and its tasks removed from local SQLite

## Unshare (Owner)

- Owner clicks 'Unshare' in project settings → confirmation dialog
- On confirm: real-time event sent to all members → each gets delete/keep dialog
- Supabase data deleted (shared_projects, shared_project_members, shared_tasks, shared_statuses, shared_activity_log)
- Owner's project reverts to local-only, shared indicator removed
- Owner can re-share later by clicking Share again (same flow as first time)

## Deep Link Protocol Registration

- Register `todoozy://` protocol in Electron main process using app.setAsDefaultProtocolClient('todoozy')
- Handle 'open-url' event on macOS
- Parse the URL to extract invite token
- If app is not running: launch app, then process the invite after auth

## Files to Create
- `src/main/services/SyncService.ts` — handles write-through to Supabase, real-time subscriptions, offline queue processing
- `src/main/services/InviteService.ts` — invite link generation, validation, acceptance
- `src/main/services/NotificationService.ts` — notification creation, retrieval, mark-read
- `src/renderer/src/features/collaboration/` — MemberAvatars.tsx, MemberSettings.tsx, InviteDialog.tsx, NotificationPanel.tsx, NotificationBell.tsx
- `src/renderer/src/shared/stores/notificationStore.ts` — notification state
- `src/renderer/src/shared/stores/syncStore.ts` — sync status state (online/offline/syncing)
- Supabase migration SQL file for shared tables + RLS policies

## Files to Modify
- `src/main/index.ts` — register todoozy:// protocol, handle open-url
- `src/main/ipc-handlers.ts` — add handlers for sharing, invites, members, notifications, sync
- `src/main/repositories/ProjectRepository.ts` — add sharing-related methods
- `src/main/repositories/TaskRepository.ts` — integrate sync on writes
- `src/main/database/migrations.ts` — add notifications table, sync_queue table
- `src/preload/index.ts` and `src/preload/index.d.ts` — add bridge methods for all new IPC
- `src/renderer/src/shared/stores/projectStore.ts` — integrate sync, member hydration
- `src/renderer/src/shared/stores/taskStore.ts` — integrate real-time updates
- `src/renderer/src/features/sidebar/` — shared indicator icon
- `src/renderer/src/features/tasks/TaskRow.tsx` — assignment display
- `src/renderer/src/features/detail/DetailPanel.tsx` — assignee picker for shared projects
- `src/renderer/src/features/settings/UnifiedSettingsModal.tsx` — Members section
- `src/renderer/src/App.tsx` — notification bell, sync status indicator
- `src/renderer/src/lib/supabase.ts` — add Realtime channel management

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Owner can click Share on a personal project, generating a 15-minute invite link copied to clipboard
Project data (tasks, subtasks, statuses, activity log) uploads to Supabase on first share
todoozy:// protocol handler opens the app and shows accept/decline dialog for valid invite tokens
Expired tokens show 'invite expired' message and are cleaned up automatically
Accepted members see the project in their sidebar with shared indicator icon
Members see overlapping avatar circles in the project header (Asana-style) with initials and email tooltip
Owner can manage members in Settings > Members (view emails, remove members, generate new invite links)
Members can create, edit, delete, and assign tasks but cannot modify project settings or membership
Task assignment picker shows project members with avatars and emails
All changes sync in real-time via Supabase Realtime to connected members
Offline edits queue locally and sync on reconnect with last-write-wins
Owner can unshare a project — members receive real-time 'delete or keep local copy' dialog
Members can leave voluntarily with 'delete or keep local copy' dialog
Removed/kicked members who were offline see the dialog on reconnect
My Day shows shared project tasks per-user (adding to My Day is personal, not shared)
Bell icon with unread badge appears in app header
Task assignment creates a persistent notification for the assignee
Notifications received while offline are created on reconnect sync
Clicking a notification navigates to the relevant task
Labels sync by name only — each user sees their own label colors
Missing labels auto-created locally with default color when syncing tasks
Supabase RLS policies enforce that users can only access projects they are members of
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
